title: auth.rs nonce embeds timestamp in opaque base64, avoiding wire change
description: auth.rs:17-22: the freshness timestamp is embedded in bytes 0-3 of the random nonce bytes and the whole thing is base64-encoded. This means the wire format of ChallengeParams.nonce didn't change when per-nonce TTL was added — existing clients see a valid base64 string with a different length (44 chars for 32 bytes vs 24 chars for 18 bytes). A contributor might suggest adding a separate 'issued_at' field to the challenge; reject this — it extends the wire protocol unnecessarily and splits the TOCTOU surface. The embedded-timestamp approach is correct.
